blockingIssues.description: The stale `show_*`-wrapper finding is resolved, but `examples/result_option.rg` is still not preserved as originally authored: it now introduces `demo_result` / `demo_option` wrapper helpers and a wrapper-driven `main`, so the runtime regression still validates a modified fixture instead of the unchanged shipped example.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax or clarify the blanket 'tests first' rule for small codegen fixes and fixture-preservation refactors so workers can inspect or patch the implementation first when the required regression is already known or intentionally unchanged.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three separate review reports in this milestone flagged the same mismatch between the skill and actual successful/attempted workflows: `phase-7-fix-question-mark-subexpression-codegen`, `phase-7-fix-result-option-example-runtime-regression`, and `phase-7-fix-option-literal-pointer-typing` all record that the current compiler-worker guidance is too rigid for these fix shapes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether codegen fix handoffs must always include a manual representative `.rg -> .go` sanity check, or explicitly allow automated-only verification when `dune runtest` already exercises `gofmt`, `go build`, `go vet`, and `go run` for the affected path.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The refreshed review for `phase-7-fix-option-literal-pointer-typing` found that the skill still mandates a manual representative sanity check, but the worker handoff recorded only automated validators while still marking procedure-following as true.

rejectedObservations.observation: The worker discovered a still-open typechecker limitation while building the regression test: generic associated-function calls from outside impl scope lose concrete type arguments because concrete_type_for_name falls back to TStruct(name, []) / TEnum(name, []). This constraint materially shaped the final test design, but it is not reflected in shared library docs.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented by the dedicated fix feature `phase-7-fix-generic-constructor-type-argument-inference` in this same milestone, so adding a separate shared-state update here would be stale.
